title: ruppia maritima var. occidentalis
intro: Ruppia maritima var. occidentalis, also known as western ruppia, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the family Potamogetonaceae, characterized by its small, submerged leaves and stems. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Ruppia maritima var. occidentalis include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Ruppia maritima var. occidentalis include teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and stems in hot water.
